title: Chapter 16: Justice
content: Aldric Thornwood's trial was held at dawn.

The great hall was packed — every wolf in the pack, from the highest noble to the lowest servant, pressed into the torchlit space to witness the judgment. Aldric stood in the center of the hall, his hands bound with silver chains, his green-tinged eyes still burning with defiance.
Kael sat on the throne. Sera stood at his right hand — not behind him, but beside him. The position of an equal. The position she had earned.

"Aldric Thornwood," Kael's voice rang through the hall, "you stand accused of treason, murder, dark magic, and conspiracy against the Ironmoon Pack. Thirty wolves died at the eastern outpost. Countless more were endangered by your rogue army. You attempted to assassinate your Alpha. You conspired with traitors within these very walls." His golden eyes were cold. "How do you plead?"
Aldric smiled. It was not the smile of a defeated man.

"I plead nothing," he said. "I do not recognize the authority of a pack that has abandoned its traditions. A pack that shelters Moon-Marked abominations. A pack that lets a wolf-less whore stand at the Alpha's right hand." His burning eyes found Sera. "You think you've won, girl? You think this is over?"
"The rogues are destroyed. Your magic is broken. Your army is ash." Sera's voice was steady. "Yes. I think we won."
"Then you're a fool."
The words hung in the air like smoke.

"The Shadow Binding was never *mine*," Aldric said, his voice dropping to something almost reverent. "I found it. In the old tombs beneath the Blackwood — the ones that predate the pack, predate the wolves, predate *everything*. Something is buried down there. Something ancient. I only borrowed its power." His smile widened. "And now that I've failed... it's going to wake up."
The hall erupted in whispers.
"Silence!" Kael's voice cut through the noise. "You're lying. Trying to spread fear with your final words."
"Am I?" Aldric's green eyes glittered. "Ask your lorekeeper. Ask Elder Magnus what's buried beneath the Blackwood. Ask him about the First Shadow. Ask him what happens when it wakes."
Sera's blood ran cold. She turned to look at Magnus, who stood at the edge of the noble section. The old wolf's face was pale — paler than she'd ever seen it.
"Magnus?" she said quietly. "Is he telling the truth?"
The silence that followed was answer enough.

"I..." Magnus's voice was unsteady. "I had hoped it was a myth. The old texts mention a darkness that predates the wolves — a being of pure shadow that was sealed beneath the forest centuries ago by the first Moon-Marked. The prophecy says 'the darkness that comes.' I thought it meant Aldric. I thought..." He shook his head. "I was wrong."

Aldric laughed — a dry, rattling sound. "The great Elder Magnus, wrong about a prophecy. How delightful. The darkness isn't *coming*, you old fool. It's already here. It's been here for centuries, sleeping. And my little magic experiment just gave it a reason to wake up."

Kael rose from his throne. "Enough. Aldric Thornwood, for your crimes against the pack, I sentence you to death. Your magic will be sealed. Your wolf will be stripped. And your name will be struck from the Thornwood line."

"You can kill me," Aldric said, still smiling. "But you can't kill what's coming. The First Shadow will rise. And when it does, your precious Silver Queen will fall — just like the first Moon-Marked fell, all those centuries ago."
Darius's blade ended the speech before it could continue.
Aldric's body crumpled to the stone floor. The green light in his eyes flickered — and went out.
But his final words lingered in the air like poison.
After the execution, Sera found Magnus in his tower.
The old wolf was surrounded by scrolls — dozens of them, unrolled across every available surface. His hands were trembling as he traced ancient script with his fingers.
"Tell me about the First Shadow," Sera said.

Magnus didn't look up. "It's in the oldest texts. The ones that predate the pack by a thousand years. Before there were wolves, there was *it*. A being of pure darkness. It consumed everything it touched — forests, animals, entire civilizations. The first Moon-Marked — a wolf whose name has been lost to history — sealed it beneath what would become the Blackwood Forest. The seal was supposed to be permanent."
"But it's not."

"The seal requires a Moon-Marked guardian. A living wolf with the crescent mark, channeling the Goddess's power into the seal to keep it closed." Magnus finally looked up, and his pale eyes were heavy with guilt. "When Aldric found the seal and began siphoning its power for his Shadow Binding, he weakened it. And now that he's dead..." He swallowed. "There's no guardian. No one channeling power into the seal."
"Except me," Sera said quietly.

Magnus nodded. "The prophecy said 'the Silver Queen and the Alpha of Two Hearts shall face the darkness that comes.' I thought Aldric was the darkness. I was wrong. Aldric was just... a symptom. The real darkness is still down there. Waiting."
"How long do we have?"

"I don't know. Days. Weeks. The seal is crumbling, but it's been holding for a thousand years — it won't break overnight." Magnus reached for her hand. "You're the first Moon-Marked in centuries, Seraphina. The seal recognized you — that's why your birthmark burned, why your wolf woke when it did. You were always meant to be its guardian. The Goddess chose you for this."
"But I'm not strong enough to fight an ancient shadow entity."
"Not yet." Magnus's grip tightened. "But you're not alone. The Alpha is bound to you. The pack is behind you. And the prophecy is clear: together, you will prevail." He paused. "You have to."
That night, Sera and Kael stood together on the battlements again.
The moon was a thin crescent now — waxing, slowly climbing back toward fullness. The Blackwood Forest stretched below them, dark and ancient, hiding secrets that had been buried for a thousand years.
"An ancient shadow entity," Kael said. "That's... bigger than I expected."

"Bigger than any of us expected." Sera leaned against the stone wall. "Magnus says I'm supposed to be its guardian. The Moon-Marked who keeps the seal closed. But I don't know how. The first Moon-Marked died sealing it. How am I supposed to do better?"

"Because you're not doing it alone." Kael turned to face her. "The first Moon-Marked didn't have a bound Alpha. Didn't have a pack that accepted her. Didn't have a prophecy foretelling her victory." His hand found hers. "You have all of that. And you have me."
"What if it's not enough?"

"Then we make it enough." His voice was iron. "We've already beaten Aldric. We've already survived poison and rogues and a trial by combat. We've already done half a dozen things that should have been impossible." He lifted her hand to his lips. "An ancient shadow entity is just one more impossible thing."
Sera managed a smile. "Fenris has a plan, doesn't he?"

*"I ALWAYS have a plan. The plan is: we find the seal, we figure out how to reinforce it, we kill whatever comes out of it if reinforcing doesn't work. Simple. Elegant. Foolproof. Well, maybe not FOOLPROOF. But definitely WOLF-PROOF. Which is the best kind of proof."*
"He says it's wolf-proof," Sera translated.

"Naturally." Kael pulled her close. "We'll start tomorrow. Magnus will show us the seal. We'll figure out how to strengthen it. And if that doesn't work — " his arms tightened around her — "we'll fight. The way we've always fought. Together."
"Together," Sera agreed.
The crescent moon hung overhead like a silver smile. The Blackwood Forest rustled with secrets.
And somewhere deep beneath the ancient trees, in a darkness that had waited a thousand years for this moment, something stirred.
